intro: Exochaenium pumilum, also known as the dwarf Exochaenium, is a low-growing, perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the family Rubiaceae. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Exochaenium pumilum include iridoid glycosides, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Exochaenium pumilum is commonly used to make herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
